name: Missouri SNAP Benefits
description: Missouri's SNAP program provides monthly food benefits on an EBT card through the Department of Social Services. Benefits help low-income individuals and families purchase groceries at authorized retailers.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 130% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: Missouri MO HealthNet (Medicaid)
description: Missouri's Medicaid program providing health coverage to eligible low-income residents. Covers doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, mental health, dental, and vision services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant individuals, seniors, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL; children up to 300% FPL; pregnant individuals up to 196% FPL

name: Missouri Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Benefits are calculated based on previous wages and can last up to 20 weeks.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own with sufficient earnings history
incomeLimitNotes: Weekly benefit based on previous wages; maximum weekly benefit applies

name: Missouri Housing Development Commission (MHDC)
description: Provides affordable housing programs including homebuyer assistance, rental assistance, and homelessness prevention. Administers tax credits, HOME funds, and Housing Trust Fund programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income homebuyers and renters in Missouri
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally targets households below 80% of area median income

name: Legal Aid of Western Missouri
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income residents in western Missouri including the Kansas City metro area. Handles cases involving housing, family law, consumer protection, and public benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals in western Missouri with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Legal Services of Eastern Missouri
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income residents in eastern Missouri including the St. Louis metro area. Handles cases involving housing, family law, domestic violence, and benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals in eastern Missouri with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Missouri Job Centers
description: Missouri's network of workforce development centers providing free employment services including job search, career counseling, resume writing, skills assessments, and training opportunities.
eligibilitySummary: All Missouri job seekers and employers

name: Missouri LIHEAP - Energy Assistance
description: Provides financial assistance to low-income households to help pay heating and cooling bills through community action agencies. Includes regular energy assistance and crisis assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households at or below 135% of the federal poverty level
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 135% FPL

name: Missouri 211
description: Free, confidential service connecting Missourians to local resources for food, housing, health care, utilities, and other essential services. Available 24/7 by phone and online.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Missouri seeking information about community resources
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Harvesters - The Community Food Network
description: A Feeding America member food bank serving northwestern Missouri and northeastern Kansas. Distributes food through over 760 partner agencies including food pantries, shelters, and soup kitchens.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in northwestern Missouri and northeastern Kansas

name: St. Louis Area Foodbank
description: A Feeding America member food bank serving 26 counties in eastern Missouri and southwestern Illinois. Distributes food through over 600 partner agencies.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in the St. Louis region

name: Ozarks Food Harvest
description: A Feeding America member food bank serving 28 counties in southwest Missouri. Distributes food through over 270 partner agencies including food pantries, meal programs, and shelters.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in southwestern Missouri

name: Missouri Veterans Commission
description: Serves Missouri veterans and their families with benefits counseling, claims assistance, and access to state veterans' homes, cemeteries, and other programs.
eligibilitySummary: Missouri veterans and their dependents

name: Missouri Crisis Line
description: Provides 24/7 crisis support for anyone in Missouri experiencing mental health crisis, suicidal thoughts, or emotional distress. Connects callers with local crisis services and resources.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Missouri experiencing a mental health or substance use crisis
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week

name: Missouri Child Care Subsidy Program
description: Provides child care assistance to eligible families through the Department of Social Services. Helps pay for licensed child care while parents work, attend school, or participate in training.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children under 13 who are working or in approved activities
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 138% FPL to enter; can continue at higher levels

name: Missouri TANF - Temporary Assistance
description: Provides temporary cash assistance and support services to low-income families with children. Includes employment preparation, job search, and supportive services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with dependent children under 18
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income limits; varies by family size

name: Missouri WIC Program
description: Provides nutritious foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and healthcare referrals for pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant or postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of the federal poverty level

name: Kansas City Area Transportation Authority (KCATA)
description: Operates bus service in the Kansas City metropolitan area including the zero-fare RideKC system. Provides accessible transportation options for all riders at no cost.
eligibilitySummary: All Kansas City area residents; zero-fare service on all routes
hours: Service varies by route; customer service Mon-Fri 6:00 AM - 7:00 PM

name: Metro Transit (St. Louis MetroLink/MetroBus)
description: Operates bus and light rail service in the St. Louis metropolitan area. Offers reduced fare programs for seniors, people with disabilities, and Medicare card holders.
eligibilitySummary: St. Louis metro area residents; reduced fares for seniors, disabled, and Medicare holders
hours: Service varies by route; customer service Mon-Fri 7:00 AM - 6:00 PM

name: St. Louis City Housing Authority
description: Provides affordable housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) to low-income families in the City of St. Louis. Manages public housing developments and rental assistance programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in St. Louis City
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 50% of area median income for Section 8

name: Kansas City Community Action Network
description: Community action agency serving the Kansas City area providing emergency services, energy assistance, housing assistance, weatherization, and self-sufficiency programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in the Kansas City area
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally at or below 200% FPL

name: Missouri Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free energy efficiency improvements to low-income homes including insulation, air sealing, and heating/cooling system repairs to reduce energy costs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters at or below 200% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Missouri Vocational Rehabilitation
description: Provides services to help people with disabilities prepare for, obtain, and maintain employment. Includes career counseling, training, job placement, and assistive technology.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Access Missouri Financial Assistance Program
description: Missouri's state financial aid program providing need-based grants to Missouri residents attending eligible colleges and universities. Covers tuition costs with no repayment required.
eligibilitySummary: Missouri residents attending eligible institutions with demonstrated financial need
incomeLimitNotes: Must demonstrate financial need as determined by FAFSA; expected family contribution limits apply

name: Missouri Adult Education
description: Provides free classes for adults including GED preparation, English language learning, basic literacy, and workforce readiness at locations across Missouri.
eligibilitySummary: Adults without a high school diploma or needing English language skills

name: 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline - Missouri
description: Provides free, confidential support for anyone in suicidal crisis or emotional distress. Call or text 988 to connect with a trained counselor 24/7.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing suicidal thoughts, mental health crisis, or emotional distress
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services in 150+ languages

name: Missouri Coalition Against Domestic and Sexual Violence
description: Provides crisis support, safety planning, and referrals for survivors of domestic and sexual violence. Connects callers with local shelters and advocacy services.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing domestic violence, dating violence, or sexual assault
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Springfield Community Center / Community Partnership of the Ozarks
description: Provides comprehensive community services in the Springfield area including housing assistance, food pantry, utility assistance, employment services, and family support programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in the Springfield/Ozarks area

name: Missouri MO HealthNet for Kids (CHIP)
description: Missouri's Children's Health Insurance Program providing health coverage for uninsured children in families that earn too much for regular Medicaid but cannot afford private insurance.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 with family income up to 300% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Family income up to 300% of the federal poverty level

name: Missouri Protection and Advocacy Services
description: Provides free legal advocacy and information to people with disabilities in Missouri. Handles issues including abuse, neglect, discrimination, education, and access to services.
eligibilitySummary: People with disabilities in Missouri
